2606.06741.title: OpenSkill: Open-World Self-Evolution for LLM Agents
2606.06741.paper_summary: Self-evolving agents requires adaptation after deployment, but existing approaches assume a usable learning loop, such as curated skills, successful trajectories, or verifier signals. Real open-world deployments may provide none of these, offering only a task prompt. In this work, we study open-world self-evolution, where an agent must build both its skills and its own verification signals from scratch, using open-world resources but no target-task supervision. We propose OpenSkill, a framework that bootstraps this loop: it acquires grounded knowledge and verification anchors from documentation, repositories, and the web, synthesizes them into transferable skills, and refines those skills against self-built virtual tasks grounded in the anchors rather than in target answers. The open world thus supplies both the knowledge to be learned and a supervision-independent practice environment, with target-task supervision reserved for final evaluation. Across three benchmarks and two target agents, OpenSkill attains the best automated pass rate while satisfying the no-supervision constraint. Analysis shows its skills transfer across models without model-specific adaptation, and its self-built verifier aligns with ground-truth outcomes despite never accessing them.
2606.06741.paper_summary_zh: 自我進化的代理在部署後需要適應，但現有的方法假設有可用的學習循環，例如策劃的技能、成功的軌跡或驗證信號。真實的開放世界部署可能沒有這些，只提供一個任務提示。在這項工作中，我們研究開放世界的自我進化，其中代理必須從零開始建立自己的技能和驗證信號，使用開放世界資源但不進行目標任務的監督。我們提出了OpenSkill，一個啟動這個循環的框架：它從文檔、庫和網絡中獲取基礎知識和驗證錨，將它們合成為可轉移的技能，並根據自建的虛擬任務對這些技能進行精煉，這些虛擬任務是基於錨而不是基於目標答案。因此，開放世界提供了要學習的知識和一個獨立於監督的實踐環境，目標任務的監督僅保留用於最終評估。在三個基準和兩個目標代理中，OpenSkill達到了最佳的自動通過率，同時滿足無監督的限制。分析顯示其技能在模型之間轉移，而無需特定於模型的適應，其自建的驗證器與真實結果對齊，儘管從未訪問過這些結果。
2606.06741.author: Zhiling Yan et.al.
2606.06741.authors: Zhiling Yan, Dingjie Song, Hanrong Zhang, Wei Liang, Yuxuan Zhang, Yutong Dai, Lifang He, Philip S. Yu, Ran Xu, Xiang Li, Lichao Sun
